name: list_datasets
description: List available datasets in the workspace with name, format, and source ID — used to decide which dataset to load

source.description: Filter by source type or origin

name: load_dataset
description: Load a dataset from file or seed data into the workspace

source_id.description: Unique source identifier for the dataset

name.description: Display name for the dataset in the workspace

name: save_dataset
description: Persist the current state of a dataset — saves all tables and mutations to durable storage

name: execute_sql
description: Execute a raw SQL query when no specialized tool fits — SELECT, aggregate, filter, or join using SQL syntax. Prefer specialized tools when available: render_chart for visualization, dimension_breakdown for metric decomposition, pivot_tables for reshaping, compare_metrics for statistical tests

query.description: SQL query to execute against loaded tables

table.description: Primary table name for the query context

name: execute_python
description: Execute arbitrary Python code against a loaded table (available as 'df') — for complex transformations, custom formulas, or operations that no specialized tool handles. Prefer format_column for standard formatting, flash_fill for filling nulls

code.description: Python expression or statement to execute

name: modify_column
description: Change values across one or more entire columns — bulk column-level transformations like normalizing all emails, applying a multiplier to a price column, or zeroing out a field. Operates on every row in the column(s)

column.description: Column name or comma-separated column names

value.description: New value or expression to apply

condition.description: Row filter — only update rows matching this condition

name: modify_row
description: Change values across one or more entire rows — fix specific records like correcting a customer's address in row 42 or updating all fields for a given ID. Operates on every column in the row(s)

row.description: Row selector — index, range ('3:7'), or condition ('name == "Alice"')

is_header.description: When true, treats the row as a header row — renames column labels instead of modifying data values

name: modify_cell
description: Change one or more individual cells by (row, column) coordinate — precise, surgical corrections to specific data points like fixing a typo in row 5, column 'name'

name: modify_table
description: Table-level structural changes — sort the entire table, reorder all columns, set a primary key, or clear data while keeping structure. Operates on the table as a whole, not individual rows or columns

column.description: Column for sort or index operations

name: insert_rows
description: Add new rows to a table — one row from a dict of values, or multiple rows from a list. Verification checks row-count limits, duplicate keys, and type compatibility

values.description: Column-to-value pairs for one row, or array of such dicts for multiple rows

position.description: Index to insert at (appends to end if omitted)

name: insert_columns
description: Add a new column to a table — computed from an expression, filled with a default, or empty. Verification checks column-name uniqueness and expression validity

expression.description: Python expression for computed column (e.g., 'price * quantity')

default.description: Default value for all rows (used when no expression)

position.description: Column position index (appends to right if omitted)

name: delete_rows
description: Remove rows from a table by index, range, or condition — e.g., drop rows where revenue is null, delete rows 5-10. Requires confirmation

condition.description: Row selector — index, range ('3:7'), or boolean condition ('revenue IS NULL')

keep.description: Keep matching rows (filter) or remove them (delete)

name: delete_columns
description: Remove one or more columns from a table by name or pattern — e.g., drop the temp_id column, remove all columns matching 'test_*'. Requires confirmation

pattern.description: Regex pattern to match column names (alternative to explicit list)

name: rollback_dataset
description: Undo the last dataset mutation and restore the previous state

name: cast_column
description: Cast a column to a new data type — string to date, int to float, object to category. Auto-detects parse format when possible

errors.description: How to handle values that cannot be cast

name: dedupe_single_col
description: Remove rows with duplicate values in a single column — checks one dimension of uniqueness. Use when you want unique values in one specific column regardless of what other columns contain (e.g., 'remove duplicate emails')

column.description: Column to check for duplicate values

name: dedupe_columns
description: Remove rows that are duplicates across a combination of columns — checks multi-dimensional uniqueness. A row is a duplicate only if ALL specified columns match (e.g., 'remove rows where first_name AND last_name AND email all match')

name: flash_fill
description: Fill null or empty cells using neighboring values — forward fill, backward fill, rolling average, or constant. For pattern-based filling (like Excel Flash Fill), not arbitrary Python. Use run_interpolation for column-relationship inference

column.description: Column to fill (all columns if omitted)

name: run_interpolation
description: Estimate missing values using model-based methods that look at relationships between columns — linear regression, spline curves, or nearest neighbor. Operates column-wise; use flash_fill when carrying values down from rows above

limit.description: Maximum consecutive NaN values to fill

name: replace_values
description: Find and replace values in a column — exact match, regex pattern, or case-insensitive substitution

name: validate_column
description: Check that values in a column belong to a valid set — enum constraints, allowed ranges, not-null checks, regex patterns. Flags violations; use format_column to fix formatting issues

value.description: Check parameter — range 'min,max', list 'a,b,c', regex pattern, or length limit

action.description: What to do with failing rows

name: format_column
description: Normalize values into a standard form using built-in patterns — emails, phone numbers, dates, currency. Safe, well-tested transformations with known behavior. Use format_custom for user-defined patterns

name: format_custom
description: Apply a user-defined formatting pattern — requires preview before applying and validates the pattern to prevent data corruption. Use format_column for standard types (email, phone, date)

pattern.description: Custom format pattern or template string

preview.description: Show preview of changes before applying (recommended: true)

validation.description: Regex to validate the pattern output — rejects if result does not match

name: merge_tables
description: Join two tables on shared key column(s) — left, inner, outer, or cross join. For complex multi-key or fuzzy joins. Use merge_by_key for simple foreign-key joins, append_tables for stacking rows

name: merge_by_key
description: Join two tables on a clear foreign key relationship — simpler than merge_tables, assumes a clean 1:many or 1:1 key. Use merge_tables for complex multi-key, cross, or fuzzy joins

right.description: Right table name (the 'one' side / lookup table)

key.description: Foreign key column name (must exist in both tables)

name: pivot_tables
description: Reshape table layout — pivot (rows to columns), unpivot/melt (columns to rows), or transpose. Changes structure, not data values. Use this instead of execute_sql when the user asks to pivot, unpivot, melt, or reshape

name: append_tables
description: Stack rows from one table onto another vertically (UNION) — use when combining datasets with the same columns. Different from merge_tables which joins columns side-by-side

align_by.description: How to align columns between tables

name: split_column
description: Split one column into multiple new columns by delimiter or regex pattern — e.g., 'full_name' on space becomes first_name and last_name

pattern.description: Regex pattern with capture groups (alternative to delimiter)

names.description: Names for resulting columns (auto-generated if omitted)

name: merge_columns
description: Combine multiple columns into one — concatenate with separator or apply a custom expression (e.g., first_name + ' ' + last_name → full_name)

expression.description: Python expression for custom combination (overrides separator)

name: cut_n_paste
description: Move data from one location to another — cuts from source and inserts at target. Works within or across tables

target_ref.description: Where to paste — 'column:name', 'row:5', or cell reference

name: define_metric
description: Save a named, reusable formula to the semantic layer — referenced by lookup_metric and execute_sql

name: lookup_metric
description: Look up a metric definition, formula, and source columns from the semantic layer

source.description: Filter by dataset or data source

name: apply_formula
description: Compute a new or existing column from a formula expression — evaluates the expression row-by-row and writes the result

formula.description: Python expression using column names (e.g., 'price * quantity')

name: semantic_layer
description: Describe the schema of columns within a table, all tables in a dataset, or all datasets — column names, types, descriptions, relationships, and sample values.

scope.description: Level of schema to describe — columns within a table, tables within a dataset, or all datasets

name.description: Specific table or dataset name (returns all at scope level if omitted)

name: heads_or_tails
description: Preview the first or last N rows of a table — quick glance at data shape and content without full profiling

end.description: Which end of the table to preview

name: describe_stats
description: Profile a table or column — row count, data types, null counts, unique values, summary statistics (mean, median, min, max, std)

column.description: Column to analyze (profiles full table if omitted)

metrics.description: Metrics to compute: min, max, mean, median, std, nulls, unique, dtype

name: compare_metrics
description: Run statistical comparisons between groups or variables — chi-square, ANOVA, Mann-Whitney, t-test, or distribution analysis. Use when the user asks to compare, test significance, or check if differences are meaningful. Use ab_split_test for controlled experiments

column_a.description: First column or group for comparison

column_b.description: Second column or group for comparison

group_by.description: Column to segment by for group-level comparisons

name: root_cause_analysis
description: Identify which dimensions or factors most explain a change in a metric — decomposes the change across segments and ranks contributing factors by impact. E.g., why did revenue drop last quarter?

baseline.description: Baseline period or condition for comparison (e.g., 'quarter == Q1')

current.description: Current period or condition to compare against baseline

name: dimension_breakdown
description: Decompose a metric across dimensions — contribution, share, and ranking per segment. E.g., 'break down revenue by region', 'show churn by cohort'. Use this instead of execute_sql GROUP BY when the user asks to break down, decompose, or segment a metric

aggregation.description: How to aggregate the metric per segment

top_n.description: Limit to top N segments by value

name: compute_correlation
description: Compute the correlation between two columns — Pearson for linear, Spearman for monotonic, Kendall for ordinal. Returns coefficient, p-value, and scatter context

name: render_chart
description: Create a visualization from table data — bar, line, pie, scatter, histogram, or heatmap. Use this whenever the user asks to chart, plot, graph, or visualize data, instead of execute_sql

name: modify_chart
description: Adjust chart labels and structure — title, axis labels, chart type. Use style_chart for visual properties (colors, legend, gridlines)

chart_id.description: Identifier of the chart to modify

name: style_chart
description: Adjust chart visual properties — color palette, legend position, gridline visibility. Use modify_chart for labels and chart type

chart_id.description: Identifier of the chart to style

name: compose_dashboard
description: Combine multiple charts and tables into a multi-panel layout

grid_columns.description: Number of columns in grid layout

name: apply_style
description: Apply conditional formatting to a table display — color scales, highlights, number formatting. Visual only; does not change underlying data

column.description: Column to style (all columns if omitted)

color.description: Color for highlight or scale endpoint (hex code)

name: export_dataset
description: Export a dataset to a downloadable file — CSV, Excel, JSON, or Parquet

name: summarize_content
description: Summarize a chart or table in plain language — grounded to a concrete artifact, extracts key takeaways, patterns, and implications

artifact_id.description: Identifier of the specific chart or query result

name: explain_content
description: Explain the analysis process, methodology, or reasoning behind a recent action — about process, not data

topic.description: What to explain — a method, a result, a decision, or a process step

name: search_reference
description: Search vetted FAQs and curated data analysis reference content — the unstructured equivalent of lookup_metric

name: coordinate_context
description: Read conversation history and current turn context — provides grounding for the agent's response

name: manage_memory
description: Read or write the agent's memory — L1 session scratchpad, L2 persistent user preferences, L3 unvetted business context from documents and domain knowledge

level.description: L1=session scratchpad, L2=persistent preferences, L3=business context (documents, reports, domain knowledge)

key.description: Memory key to read, write, or delete

name: read_flow_stack
description: Read the current flow stack — active flow name, slot values, flow metadata, and pending flows

operation.description: What to read from the flow stack

flow_name.description: Specific flow to query (current flow if omitted)

name: handle_ambiguity
description: Call when the user's request is ambiguous and could map to multiple operations. Signal that clarification is needed before proceeding.

clarification.description: The clarifying question to ask the user to disambiguate their request

candidates.description: List of plausible tool names that could apply

name: conversational_response
description: Call when the user's message is conversational, an acknowledgement, or does not require any data operation. Use this to respond naturally without invoking a domain tool.

reason.description: Brief explanation of why a conversational response is appropriate
